name: Ham-Bean Scallop (Usda)
recipeIngredient: 1 cup green pepper, cut in thin strips
1 1/2 quarts bean cooking liquid and milk
8 ounces processed cheddar cheese, shredded (2 cups)
recipeInstructions:
1. Line four 8x8x2-inch baking pans with heat-resistant freezer wrap, allowing enough extra wrap to fold over the top.
Use one pan for each six servings or one-fourth of the recipe.
Do not line pans for food to be served without freezing.
2. Add beans to boiling water; return to boiling and boil 2 minutes.
Then soak beans for 1 hour (or overnight, if preferred).
3. Add salt to beans and cook slowly until tender, about 1 1/2 hours.
Drain the beans; save cooking liquid.
4. Cook onion and green pepper in fat for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
5. Stir in flour and mustard.
6. Add enough milk to the reserved bean liquid to make 1 1/2 quarts.
Slowly add the bean liquid and milk to the vegetable mixture.
Cook until thickened, stirring constantly.
7. Stir in beans, cheese, and ham. Pour one-fourth of the mixture into each baking pan.
Pack food tightly to avoid air pockets.
To Serve Without Freezing:
1. Preheat oven to 375° F (moderate).
2. Mix 1/4 cup fine breadcrumbs with 1 tablespoon melted butter or margarine for each pan that will not be frozen.
3. Sprinkle topping over bean mixture in the baking pan.
4. Bake for 30 minutes, or until crumbs are lightly browned.
To Freeze:
1. Cool food for 30 minutes at room temperature.
2. Complete wrapping by pulling paper up over top of food and folding several times so paper lies directly on top of the food.
3. Fold ends of freezer wrap over the top and seal with freezer tape.
4. Label with the name of the food, date of freezing, and last date for best eating quality (about 6 months).
5. Freeze immediately for 10 to 12 hours before removing packages from the pans.
To Heat Frozen Food:
1. Preheat oven to 375° F (moderate).
2. Remove freezer wrap.
3. Place food in a baking pan.
4. Mix 1/4 cup fine breadcrumbs with 1 tablespoon melted butter or margarine for each pan.
5. Sprinkle topping over bean mixture in the baking pan.
6. Bake for 1 1/2 hours, or until edges are bubbly, crumbs are lightly browned, and center is hot.
